text.title: ar-design-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ar-design-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Outdoor lighting variability breaks plane detection and color matching, especially on Quest passthrough. Objects that look correct indoors look washed out or saturated in sunlight.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Outdoor lighting varies by time of day, cloud cover, and reflection. Dynamic lighting estimation cannot keep up. Solution: test in target lighting conditions or use unlit, high-saturation shaders for outdoor AR.

failure-mode.description: Object recognition is brittle outside trained classes. Agents hallucinate that "object recognition works like human vision" but trained models only recognize the exact classes they were trained on.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CoreML / ARCore Augmented Images require custom training per object class. Generic object detection (YOLO) on mobile is too slow. Solution: define a closed set of recognizable objects and test extensively before shipping.

failure-mode.description: Battery drain: continuous tracking + render at 60 fps drains a phone in 30-45 minutes. AR is power-hungry.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: GPU and IMU tracking run continuously. Solution: cap session duration, provide clear battery warnings, and optimize render loops (use LOD, cull off-screen objects, reduce geometry count).

failure-mode.description: Privacy: AR cameras capture bystanders without consent. This raises red flags in EU (GDPR), CA (CCPA), and Israel (Privacy Law) jurisdictions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AR requires camera access, and any recording includes background people. Solution: clearly disclose camera recording, provide a "do not record" affordance in shared sessions, and avoid persistent capture.

failure-mode.description: Cross-platform parity (ARKit ↔ ARCore ↔ WebXR) is partial. Agents over-promise feature equivalence but ARKit's scene mesh, ARCore's depth sensing, and WebXR's anchors are not equivalent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each platform has different sensor hardware and SDKs. Feature parity requires per-platform implementation. Solution: define a core feature set and gracefully degrade on platforms that don't support it.

failure-mode.description: Cybersickness from misaligned overlays at >70ms latency. High latency between head movement and visual update causes nausea.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The human brain expects motion-to-photon latency under 70ms. Mobile ARCore/ARKit typically achieve 50-100ms, but network latency, per-frame render time, or poor frame pacing breaks this. Solution: target 90 fps on high-end devices, profile end-to-end latency, and implement frame pacing.

failure-mode.description: Agents conflate ARKit world tracking with ARCore. Features like "scene mesh" are not equivalent across platforms.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ARKit (iOS) has scene mesh understanding; ARCore (Android) has depth sensing. Different capabilities, different APIs. Solution: test on each target platform.

failure-mode.description: LLMs hallucinate Reality Composer Pro APIs. Ground via current Apple sample code, not guesses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reality Composer Pro is newer and less well-documented in training data. Solution: provide agents with Apple's sample code or Reality Composer documentation.

failure-mode.description: Agents underestimate device thermal throttling. Ship with low-poly + LOD by default.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 60 fps AR on a phone can cause the device to thermally throttle after 15 minutes. Solution: profile on target devices, use LOD meshes, and cap frame rate if needed.

failure-mode.description: Camera + microphone permissions are PII gates. Agents must produce a privacy disclosure string.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Requesting camera/microphone access is a trust breach. Users expect clear privacy disclosures. Solution: generate privacy policy text and obtain explicit user consent.

failure-mode.description: Bystander capture: agent must include a "do not record" affordance in shared sessions. Recording bystanders without consent is illegal in many jurisdictions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any AR app with camera access may record bystanders. Solution: provide explicit "recording off" toggle and legal disclaimers.

failure-mode.description: Never let an agent auto-publish AR content for kids without COPPA/GDPR-K review. AR for children has special legal and ethical constraints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: COPPA (USA) and GDPR Article 8 (EU) restrict collection from children under 13/16. Solution: block auto-publish for kid-targeting content and require legal review.
